name: ble

documentation.readme: ble is a Golang Bluetooth Low Energy package for Linux and Mac OS.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_bluetooth_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Bluetooth category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_bluetooth_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Bluetooth system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category
function_signatures.description: Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_teaching_example_in_the_prompt_stanza_so_the_llm_can_reason_about_the_right_run_order
function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for ble (go-ble/ble). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for ble. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with tinygo-org/bluetooth (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with paypal/gatt (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with graipher/bluewalker (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: [![GoDoc](https://godoc.org/github.com/go-ble/ble?status.svg)](https://godoc.org/github.com/go-ble/ble)
[![Go Report Card](https://goreportcard.com/badge/go-ble/ble)](https://goreportcard.com/report/go-ble/ble)
[![codebe.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: ble is a Golang Bluetooth Low Energy package for Linux and Mac OS.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `ble` from its upstream repository, then run `ble --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases:
When you need Bluetooth, use ble because [![GoDoc](https://godoc.org/github.com/go-ble/ble?status.svg)](https://godoc.org/github.com/go-ble/ble)
[![Go Report Card](https://goreportcard.com/badge/go-ble.
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair ble with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ble --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
